resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def findUnsortedSubarray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        sorted_nums = sorted(nums)
        left, right = n, 0
        for i in range(n):
            if nums[i] != sorted_nums[i]:
                left = min(left, i)
                right = max(right, i)
        return right - left + 1 if right >= left else 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution first sorts the input array and then iterates through both the original and sorted arrays simultaneously. It identifies the first and last indices where the elements differ, marking the start and end of the unsorted subarray. The length of this subarray is returned.  The use of an additional sorted array consumes O(n) space.
